title: Convert Influence Selection to Dropdown with Context Label
story: As a data analyst, I want the influence selection for linked services to be a dropdown with a clear 'Has Influence On' label, so that I can intuitively set metric influence relationships.
description:
The Linked Service UX for setting metric influence was updated:

1. **Influence select box** converted from a checkbox/text input to a dropdown control
2. **Default state**: deselected (no influence assigned)
3. **Dropdown title**: "Has Influence On" for clarity
4. **Context**: The selected metric name is restated in the influence UI for reference (e.g., showing "Activations" as the metric being influenced)
acceptance_criteria: Influence selection uses a dropdown control instead of a text/checkbox input
Dropdown has the title 'Has Influence On'
The currently selected metric name is displayed in the influence UI for context
ux_changes: Replaced the influence select box with a dropdown titled 'Has Influence On', deselected by default. The selected metric name is restated for context in the influence linking interface.
comment_summary: Developer completed the change and deployed to staging for review. No issues reported.
